title: AWS Solutions Architect Associate
description: AWS Solutions Architect Associate practice deck with concise scenario-based questions for study and exam sessions.

questions.question: A team needs temporary access to AWS resources without sharing long-term credentials. Which approach is best?
answers: Use IAM roles with temporary credentials
Embed access keys in application code
Store the root account password in a vault
questions.explanation: IAM roles provide temporary credentials and avoid the risks of distributing long-term access keys.

questions.question: A company stores sensitive files in Amazon S3 and wants encryption keys managed by AWS KMS. Which option should it use?
questions.explanation: SSE-KMS encrypts S3 objects while allowing key control and auditing through AWS KMS.

questions.question: Instances in private subnets need access to Amazon S3 without traversing the public internet. What should be added?
answers: A NAT instance with a public IP
A gateway VPC endpoint for S3
A peering connection to another VPC
questions.explanation: A gateway VPC endpoint lets private resources reach S3 over the AWS network without internet exposure.

questions.question: An application running on EC2 must retrieve database credentials securely and support automatic rotation. Which service is the best fit?
answers: AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store standard strings
questions.explanation: AWS Secrets Manager is designed for secrets storage and automated credential rotation.

questions.question: Which control acts as a stateful firewall for an Amazon EC2 instance?
questions.explanation: Security groups are stateful and evaluate traffic at the instance level.

questions.question: A central security account must audit resources in multiple AWS accounts. Which design scales best?
answers: Create duplicate IAM users in every account
Share the root credentials across accounts
Open all resources to the public internet
questions.explanation: Cross-account IAM roles provide controlled access across accounts without duplicating users or sharing credentials.

questions.question: A web application must remain available if one Availability Zone fails. Which design should be used?
answers: One EC2 instance in one subnet
Instances across multiple Availability Zones behind a load balancer
A larger EC2 instance in a single AZ
One EBS volume shared by all users
questions.explanation: Spreading instances across multiple AZs behind a load balancer improves availability during an AZ outage.

questions.question: Which service is best for storing backups with high durability and regional resilience?
questions.explanation: Amazon S3 is designed for highly durable object storage and is a common choice for backups.

questions.question: Which service automatically launches or removes EC2 instances based on demand or health checks?
questions.explanation: AWS Auto Scaling adjusts capacity automatically to maintain availability and match demand.

questions.question: A system needs to decouple application components so temporary spikes do not overwhelm downstream services. Which service should be used?
questions.explanation: Amazon SQS buffers messages between components, which helps absorb bursts and decouple workloads.

questions.question: A mission-critical relational database needs automated standby failover in another Availability Zone. Which option should be chosen?
questions.explanation: RDS Multi-AZ provides a standby instance in another AZ and supports automatic failover.

questions.question: What S3 feature helps recover objects that were accidentally overwritten or deleted?
questions.explanation: S3 versioning preserves previous object versions, making accidental deletion or overwrite easier to recover from.

questions.question: Which AWS service can improve read performance for frequently accessed application data with in-memory caching?
questions.explanation: Amazon ElastiCache stores data in memory, reducing latency for repeated reads.

questions.question: Which service should be used to reduce latency for users downloading static content globally?
questions.explanation: CloudFront caches content at edge locations closer to users around the world.

questions.question: A database-backed application has heavy read traffic. Which RDS feature can help offload read queries from the primary instance?
questions.explanation: RDS Read Replicas scale read-heavy workloads by directing read traffic away from the primary database.

questions.question: An event-driven workload has unpredictable traffic and short processing time. Which compute option is usually the best fit?
questions.explanation: Lambda is well suited to short-lived, event-driven workloads with variable demand.

questions.question: A team wants to run SQL queries directly against data stored in Amazon S3 without provisioning servers. Which service should it use?
questions.explanation: Amazon Athena lets teams query S3 data with SQL without managing infrastructure.

questions.question: Multiple Linux-based EC2 instances need shared file storage that can scale automatically. Which service is the best fit?
questions.explanation: Amazon EFS provides scalable shared file storage for multiple Linux EC2 instances.

questions.question: A company keeps large log files that are rarely accessed but must be retained for months. Which S3 approach is most cost-effective?
answers: Use an S3 lifecycle policy to transition objects to a lower-cost storage class
Keep all logs in S3 Standard forever
Store logs on EC2 instance store
questions.explanation: S3 lifecycle policies reduce storage cost by transitioning infrequently accessed data to cheaper storage classes.

questions.question: Which pricing model is usually best for predictable EC2 workloads that run continuously over time?
questions.explanation: Long-term commitment models such as Savings Plans and Reserved Instances usually lower cost for steady workloads.

questions.question: Which EC2 purchasing option can provide the lowest cost for flexible, fault-tolerant batch processing?
questions.explanation: Spot Instances offer deep discounts for interruptible workloads that can tolerate interruptions.

questions.question: Which design choice often reduces operational cost by offloading undifferentiated database administration work to AWS?
answers: Using Amazon RDS instead of self-managed databases on EC2
Installing a database manually on larger EC2 instances
Running all workloads in one Availability Zone
questions.explanation: Managed services like RDS reduce operational effort for backups, patching, and maintenance.

questions.question: Which AWS tool helps teams track spending against a defined threshold and send alerts when costs exceed that amount?
questions.explanation: AWS Budgets lets teams set cost or usage thresholds and receive notifications.

questions.question: Which AWS framework helps teams review architectures against cost optimization and other best-practice pillars?
questions.explanation: The AWS Well-Architected Framework includes the Cost Optimization pillar and other architecture best practices.
